column4: definition
The rate at which a function changes with respect to a variable
The area under a curve representing the accumulation of a quantity
A rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns
A statement that has been proven based on axioms and previously established results
An expression consisting of variables and coefficients using addition subtraction and multiplication
A quantity with both magnitude and direction
A natural number greater than one with no divisors other than one and itself
The exponent to which a base must be raised to produce a given number
A measure of the likelihood that an event will occur ranging from zero to one
An arrangement of elements in a specific order
A selection of elements from a set where order does not matter
A line that a curve approaches but never reaches
The product of all positive integers up to and including a given number
The longest side of a right triangle opposite the right angle
An equation or expression of the second degree containing a squared variable
A number indicating how many times a base is multiplied by itself
The multiplicative inverse of a number that when multiplied by the original gives one
A numerical factor multiplied by a variable in an algebraic expression
Having the same shape and size so that figures can be superimposed exactly
A line that touches a curve at exactly one point without crossing it
A number that cannot be expressed as a simple fraction of two integers
The middle value in a sorted list of numbers
The value that appears most frequently in a data set
A measure of the amount of variation or dispersion in a set of values
A symmetrical curve formed by the intersection of a cone with a parallel plane
The bottom number in a fraction representing the total number of equal parts
The top number in a fraction representing how many parts are taken
Two lines or planes meeting at a right angle of ninety degrees
The total distance around the outside of a circle
A scalar that indicates how much an eigenvector is scaled during a linear transformation
